events.title: Morning Bird Watching Tour
events.description: Join us for an early morning bird watching adventure! Our experienced guides will help you spot and identify various bird species native to the St. Margaret's Bay woodland area. Bring your binoculars and camera to capture these beautiful creatures in their natural habitat. Perfect for both beginners and experienced bird watchers.

instructor.name: Sarah Mitchell
instructor.bio: Sarah has been a bird enthusiast for over 15 years and is certified by the Audubon Society.

events.title: Native Plant Identification Workshop
events.description: Learn to identify native plants, trees, and shrubs found throughout the conservation area. This hands-on workshop will teach you about the ecological importance of native species and how they support local wildlife. Perfect for gardeners, nature enthusiasts, and anyone interested in botany.

instructor.name: Dr. James Thompson
instructor.bio: Dr. Thompson is a botanist with 20 years of experience studying Maritime flora.

events.title: Trail Maintenance Day
events.description: Help us maintain and improve our beautiful trail system! This volunteer day includes clearing brush, repairing trail markers, and general maintenance work. All tools and refreshments provided. A great way to give back to the community while getting some exercise and fresh air.

instructor.name: Mark Davidson
instructor.bio: Mark is our head trail coordinator with extensive experience in trail building and maintenance.

events.title: Fall Foliage Photography Walk
events.description: Capture the stunning autumn colors of the woodland conservation area! This guided photography walk will take you to the most scenic spots while our instructor shares tips on landscape and nature photography. All skill levels welcome, from smartphone photographers to DSLR enthusiasts.

instructor.name: Emma Rodriguez
instructor.bio: Emma is a professional nature photographer with work featured in National Geographic.

events.title: Family Nature Scavenger Hunt
events.description: A fun-filled adventure for the whole family! Teams will explore the trails while searching for items on our nature scavenger hunt list. Learn about local plants, animals, and ecosystems while having a blast. Prizes for all participants! Perfect for families with children aged 5-12.

instructor.name: Jennifer Lee
instructor.bio: Jennifer is an environmental educator specializing in family programming.

events.title: Guided Nature Walk: Winter Preparation
events.description: Discover how plants and animals prepare for the coming winter months. This educational walk will explore the fascinating adaptations that allow local flora and fauna to survive harsh winter conditions. Learn about hibernation, migration, and dormancy while enjoying a peaceful walk through the woods.

instructor.name: Robert Chen
instructor.bio: Robert is a naturalist with expertise in wildlife behavior and ecology.
